---
title: "Tutorials"
---

The tutorials are designed as a guided path from Kalah rules to AlphaZero-style
self-play.

If you are new to AI game playing, begin with
[AI Game Learning in Plain Language](00_plain_language_intro.md). It introduces
the main ideas without code or equations, so the technical chapters have a
friendlier first step.

## Learning Path

Foundations

0. [AI Game Learning in Plain Language](00_plain_language_intro.md)
1. [Kalah Rules and State Representation](01_kalah_rules.md)
2. [Perfect-Information Games](02_perfect_information_games.md)
3. [Baseline Agents](03_baseline_agents.md)

Search

4. [Monte Carlo Tree Search](04_monte_carlo_tree_search.md)
5. [From UCT to AlphaZero PUCT](05_puct.md)

Learning Intuition

6. [Neural Policy and Value Networks](06_neural_policy_value_networks.md)
7. [Self-Play Training Loop](07_self_play_training.md)
8. [AlphaZero Loss Derivation](08_alphazero_loss.md)

In Practice

9. [Evaluation and Elo-Style Comparisons](09_evaluation.md)
10. [Instrumentation and Debugging](10_instrumentation.md)
11. [Scaling Strength](11_scaling_strength.md)
12. [Reading the Whole Codebase](12_codebase_walkthrough.md)
13. [Evaluation of best checkpoint](13_residual_depth_evaluation.qmd)

## Study Rhythm

Each chapter follows the same rhythm:

$$
\text{concept}
\rightarrow
\text{equations}
\rightarrow
\text{code}
\rightarrow
\text{experiment}.
$$

For the plain-language page, read for the story. For the technical chapters,
read with the source tree open. The code excerpts are intentionally small; the
full implementation lives in `src/kalah_zero/`.
